frameworks.name: classical serial basal ganglia model
frameworks.one_line: striatal action selection precedes a pallidothalamic motor gate in serial architecture

frameworks.name: cognition-to-action binding
frameworks.one_line: actions are bound to underlying cognitive variables across pallidothalamic circuit

frameworks.name: population decoding of neural representations
frameworks.one_line: decoding choice and confidence from single-neuron populations along the circuit

frameworks.name: unsigned reward prediction error
frameworks.one_line: motor thalamus converges confidence and outcome into unsigned RPE-like signal

core_question: How does the human pallidothalamic circuit bind cognitive variables (choice, confidence, outcome) to action?
key_assumptions: single-neuron recordings in human basal ganglia-thalamic nuclei index circuit-level computation
confidence-weighted reaching reports both perceptual decision and confidence
population-level decoding reveals representational transformations along the circuit
manipulations: perceptual decision task with confidence-weighted reaching
recording across globus pallidus externus, internus, subthalamic nucleus, and motor thalamus
alignment of activity to movement onset, execution, and feedback
key_findings: neurons across the pallidothalamic circuit show sharp activity increases at movement onset persisting through execution and feedback
choice and confidence are continuously encoded before, during, and after movement
representations transform from dynamic upstream to stable low-dimensional downstream, especially in motor thalamus
outcome signals emerge selectively in subthalamic nucleus and motor thalamus after feedback
interpretations: the circuit binds cognition to action rather than implementing a serial action-selection-then-gate architecture
motor thalamus converges confidence and outcome into an unsigned reward prediction error representation
